sections.paragraph:
τυφλόω (τυφλός) 1 aor. ἐτύφλωσα; pf. τετύφλωκα; aor. pass. 3 sg. ἐτυφλώθη,-θησαν LXX (Pind., Hdt. et al.; CB I/2, 386 no. 232, 15; PLond V, 1708, 84; LXX; TestSol 12:2 P; Test12Patr) to deprive of sight, to blind in our lit. only in imagery (Is 42:19) τετύφλωκεν αὐτῶν τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς (cp. TestDan 2:4) J 12:40 (cp. Is 6:10).—1J 2:11; or fig. τυφ. τὰ νοήματα 2 Cor 4:4 (Pla., Phd. 99e μὴ τὴν ψυχὴν τυφλωθείην; Herm. Wr. 478, 32 Sc.; Philo, Ebr. 108 διάνοιαν τυφλωθείς; Jos., Ant. 8, 30; Test Sim 2:7 ὁ ἄρχων τῆς πλάνης ἐτύφλωσέ μου τὸν νοῦν).—DELG s.v. τύφομαι B. M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. sg. = singular

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

CB Ramsay, CB = The Cities and Bishoprics of Phrygia

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

TestDan TestDan = Testament of Dan, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1021
